name: Raleigh to Oklahoma City LTL
hero-headline: Raleigh, NC to Oklahoma City, OK LTL Freight
subheadline: 1,250 miles. 4-5 business day transit. 7 carriers active on this lane.
body-content:
Lane Overview: Raleigh to Oklahoma City

The Raleigh to Oklahoma City LTL lane covers approximately 1,250 miles between the Southeast and South Central regions. Standard transit runs 4-5 business days depending on carrier routing and terminal schedules. Common equipment on this corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Cross-dock facilities at Charlotte Terminal, Philadelphia Terminal, Atlanta Hub support consolidation and transit optimization. Hurricane risk Jun-Nov. Consistent retail demand year-round.

How WARP Operates the Raleigh to Oklahoma City Lane

WARP covers the Raleigh to Oklahoma City corridor with 7 vetted carriers providing LTL capacity.

  • Service capabilities on this lane include: Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery; Estimated 4-5 business day transit window; Pallet-level tracking with delivery appointment scheduling.
  • Shipments receive pallet-level tracking from pickup through delivery with appointment scheduling and delivery confirmation.
  • Nearby cross-dock operations at Charlotte Terminal and Philadelphia Terminal enable consolidation and re-routing when exceptions occur.
  • Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change.
  • WARP's operations team manages carrier coordination, rescheduling, and resolution without requiring shipper follow-up.

Operating Details

Key operational considerations for this lane:

  • Estimated lane distance: 1,250 miles (Raleigh to Oklahoma City)
  • Transit window: 4-5 business days for standard LTL service
  • Available equipment: Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled)
  • Active carriers on this lane: 7
  • Nearest cross-dock facilities: Charlotte Terminal, Philadelphia Terminal, Atlanta Hub, Dallas Mega Hub, Kansas City Terminal
  • Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery milestones
  • Exception alerts within 30 minutes of status changes with automated escalation
  • Delivery appointment scheduling and confirmation managed by WARP operations

Pricing & Rate Factors

Estimated LTL rates on the Raleigh to Oklahoma City lane range from $4,958 to $6,610, depending on freight class, weight, pallet count, and seasonal demand.

  • Key cost drivers include NMFC freight classification, shipment density, and whether accessorials such as liftgate, inside delivery, or limited-access pickup apply.
  • At 1,250 miles, the linehaul distance is the largest cost component (approximately $4.
  • 63/mile).
  • Fuel surcharges are applied as a separate line item based on current diesel prices.
  • These are modeled estimates.

Enter your shipment details to get a real-time, all-in rate.

Validate This Lane

Validate this lane with a controlled pilot: Raleigh to Oklahoma City LTL (1,250-mile corridor; 4–5 day transit window; 7 vetted carriers active; cross-dock access at Charlotte Terminal and Philadelphia Terminal). Track quote response time, transit predictability, and exception trends before scaling volume. Start with a single shipment to benchmark WARP against your current process.

Metro hubs: Raleigh Freight Hub · Oklahoma City Freight Hub

faq-schema:
How much does LTL freight from Raleigh, NC to Oklahoma City, OK cost?

Estimated LTL rates range from $4,958 to $6,610 based on freight class, weight, and pallet count. Actual rates depend on shipment specifics and current market conditions. Get an instant quote for real-time pricing.

What is the transit time for LTL shipments from Raleigh to Oklahoma City?

Standard LTL transit on this 1,250-mile lane is 4-5 business days. Actual transit depends on carrier routing, terminal schedules, and pickup timing.

What carriers does WARP use on the Raleigh to Oklahoma City lane?

WARP maintains a vetted network of approximately 7 active carriers on this corridor. Carrier selection is optimized for each shipment based on equipment requirements, transit targets, and performance history.

Can I track my LTL shipment from Raleigh to Oklahoma City in real time?

Yes. Every shipment includes real-time tracking with scan events at pickup, in-transit checkpoints, and delivery. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change, and WARP's operations team manages resolution directly.

What equipment is available for LTL freight on this lane?

Common equipment on the Raleigh to Oklahoma City corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Equipment availability varies by season and demand. Specify your requirements when requesting a quote.
